mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in industrial production reflects expanding corporate output and earnings capacity, which drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces negative twenty-day returns in more than fifty percent of months when INDPRO is rising, or the Rank IC between INDPRO changes and forward returns stays below 0.03 over any three-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate sustained above 2.0 percent signals restrictive monetary policy that raises discount rates and borrowing costs, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next sixty trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive sixty-day returns in more than fifty-five percent of instances when FEDFUNDS exceeds 2.0 percent, or the Rank IC remains above zero over any two-year window during such episodes.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in the unemployment rate and decreases in nonfarm payroll employment jointly signal labor market contraction that single series alone do not capture, predicting negative S&P 500 returns over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: Instances with both rising UNRATE and falling PAYEMS fail to show negative twenty-day S&P 500 returns in at least fifty percent of cases, and the interaction rank correlation is not significantly negative (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales predict positive S&P 500 returns over the next five trading days when the unemployment rate is below 4.0 percent, as consumer demand strength translates into immediate earnings optimism in a tight labor market.
failure_condition: When UNRATE is below 4.0 percent, positive month-over-month RSAFS growth coincides with negative five-day S&P 500 returns in more than forty-five percent of instances, or the Rank IC falls below 0.02 over any two-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in nonfarm payrolls signals durable income and consumption trajectories, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next sixty trading days.
failure_condition: Positive month-over-month changes in PAYEMS fail to predict positive sixty-day returns with a Rank IC above 0.03 over any three-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in consumer prices combined with a month-over-month decrease in industrial production signals stagflationary margin compression, jointly predicting negative S&P 500 returns over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: Joint occurrences of rising CPIAUCSL and falling INDPRO fail to produce negative twenty-day returns in more than fifty-five percent of instances, or the interaction Rank IC is not negative.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts reflect durable construction activity and household formation that supports cyclical demand, predicting positive S&P 500 returns over the next sixty trading days.
failure_condition: Positive month-over-month changes in HOUST fail to correlate with positive sixty-day S&P 500 returns, yielding a Rank IC below 0.03 over any three-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Back-to-back monthly declines in industrial production signal a deepening manufacturing contraction that quickly translates into negative earnings revisions, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next five trading days.
failure_condition: Two consecutive monthly declines in INDPRO do not produce negative five-day S&P 500 returns in more than fifty percent of triggered instances.
